notes: Coal Briquette is produced at a 1:1 refining ratio from coal via the Refinery 7.5 m. Unlike the uranium→enriched-uranium chain (100:1 by mass), briquetting doesn't concentrate energy — coal has roughly the same MJ/kg before and after briquetting. The gain is combustion efficiency: briquettes burn cleanly in the Combustion Generator 2.5 m at ~40% electrical efficiency vs ~15% for raw coal. Ash is produced when briquettes burn; its usedIn note forward-references Stone & Concrete's Cement recipe as a future fly-ash additive (real-world pozzolan). No hard dependency — Stone & Concrete's cement recipe is unchanged.

resources.description: Compressed coal dust bonded with pitch. Briquetting reshapes coal for clean, consistent combustion — the refined form yields roughly 2.7× the effective electrical output of raw coal in the Combustion Generator 2.5 m. The gain is a combustion-efficiency gain, not a mass gain (1 coal → 1 briquette, ~15% → ~40% electrical efficiency), matching real-world coal-power economics.

Fine mineral residue left after briquettes are consumed in the Combustion Generator 2.5 m. Forward-looking: real-world fly ash is a supplementary cementitious material (pozzolan) — a future revision of Stone & Concrete's Cement recipe can optionally accept ash alongside stone powder. In v0.1 this is metadata only; the Stone & Concrete mod is not modified.
resources.producedBy: Combustion Generator 2.5 m (combustion byproduct, 1 per briquette consumed)
